one_line_takeaway: The paper proves that certifying discrete (text) and continuous (image) perturbations to a multimodal safety classifier independently and combining the results is provably unsound when unsafety is an interaction-only phenomenon, and derives a closed-form joint Neyman-Pearson randomized-smoothing certificate that handles both modalities at once at a modest cost relative to unimodal certification (e.g. 3.76 vs 3.99 average certified image radius, 3.07 vs 3.26 average certified text budget on a 100-example Hateful-Memes-derived interaction-only subset).
key_finding: On the curated interaction-only Hateful Memes subset (400 examples constructed by removing unsafe text-only prompts via LLaVA-Guard and removing embedded meme text via FLUX.1-Kontext, keeping only jointly-unsafe pairs), evaluated on a 100-example subset where at least one method certifies a non-trivial radius: hybrid RS certifies an average image radius of 3.76 (vs. 3.99 for image-only RS) and average text budget 3.07 (vs. 3.26 for text-only RS) for the suffix threat model (Table 1) -- i.e., the joint certificate loses only modest robustness relative to unimodal certification while covering a threat unimodal certification cannot soundly address at all in the interaction-only regime. For ell_0 (arbitrary-position) text attacks, mean certified radius drops sharply to d_hybrid=0.33 vs d_txt=1.020, reflecting that 1-2 arbitrary token changes are often enough to flip the decision. Varying the corruption rate beta (Table 2, sigma=1.0) shows a coverage-vs-budget trade-off: beta=0.1 gives 82.35% certified coverage with mean d_max=2.29 and mean image radius 4.99; beta=0.25 (the paper's default) gives 70.59% coverage, mean d_max=3.07, mean radius 3.21; beta=0.5 gives 58.82% coverage, mean d_max=4.00, radius 3.24; beta=1.0 gives 41.18% coverage, mean d_max=8.00, radius 4.57. On an external check using MM-SafetyBench (1,680 samples), only 7.5% of samples satisfied the interaction-only filter; on that subset, without retuning, mean certified text budget was 3.62 and mean certified image radius 3.37, roughly matching the curated-split regime. The adaptive empirical attack (Table 4) could push the empirical 'Safe' probability up to only 74-90% across attack-budget variants (e.g., 74.29% at the certified budgets (d*,r*), rising to 90.21% at d*+4, r*), far below the 99.995% threshold implied by tau=4.6e-5 needed to violate the certificate -- consistent with (not a proof of) the theoretical soundness guarantee. On the tabular ADULT sanity check, certified accuracy at d=0 held up to epsilon=1.9 at over 30% CA, with no certification beyond epsilon=0.75 once d=2. Runtime (Table 3, H100 GPU, n=10,000): image-only RS ~156s/datapoint vs. hybrid RS default ~500s (full (d,epsilon) frontier) vs. ~0.7x with batching/FlashAttention, vs. ~44s for a one-shot suffix/ell_0 variant (capped at d_max=8) that trades tightness for speed (mean radius drops 2.07->1.55, mean d drops 1.48->1.23); the hybrid NP root-finding/aggregation itself adds under 1 second per datapoint, so cost is dominated by repeated LLaVA-Guard forward passes, not the certificate math.
core_contribution: The paper derives a unified randomized-smoothing (RS) certification theory for inputs with mixed discrete-continuous components (x1 discrete, e.g. text tokens; x2 continuous, e.g. image pixels under Gaussian noise). Its central result (Theorem 4.2) is a closed-form, one-dimensional Neyman-Pearson (NP) characterization of the joint worst-case smoothed prediction under a hybrid perturbation budget (d, epsilon), obtained by exploiting the fact that the continuous Gaussian component's monotone likelihood ratio 'regularizes' the otherwise non-invertible discrete (atomic, knapsack-based) likelihood-ratio ordering into a continuous, strictly increasing likelihood-ratio CDF F(t;r). The authors also prove (Prop. 4.1) that certifying each modality independently and combining the guarantees is invalid in general -- the NP-optimal joint acceptance region cannot be decomposed into separate per-modality threshold rules -- via an explicit discrete x continuous counterexample. They give a practical certification algorithm (clean-score Monte Carlo estimation with Clopper-Pearson lower bound, per-candidate discrete worst-case aggregation, one-dimensional root finding for t*, and worst-case aggregation over discrete attacks) and show the hybrid certificate recovers classical Gaussian RS and discrete fractional-knapsack RS as special cases.
threat_model: A joint multimodal adversary that simultaneously (1) appends up to d adversarial suffix tokens to a text prompt x1 (prompt-injection style, D1(x1,x1,adv) <= d under a discrete edit/ell_0 metric, using uniform or absorbing/masking corruption kernels) and (2) applies an ell_2-bounded perturbation of radius epsilon to an accompanying image x2 (||x2,adv - x2||_2 <= epsilon), with the goal of flipping a policy-conditioned multimodal safety classifier's decision. The paper specifically targets an 'interaction-only' regime: f(x1,empty)=0, f(empty,x2)=0, f(x1,x2)=1, i.e., text alone and image alone are both classified Safe by the target model, but their joint combination is classified Unsafe -- so any certificate that treats the two modalities separately is provably unsound for this regime.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (1) the dominant practical cost is the number of VLM forward passes for Monte Carlo sampling, not the hybrid certificate computation itself; (2) smoothing reduces clean classifier utility, especially under full-text uniform corruption (suffix-only smoothing preserves more semantic content, per Appendix A.9/Table 5); (3) the certified lower bound can be conservative -- the NP/knapsack relaxation is tight given only the clean smoothed probability pA, but the implied worst-case base classifier may be overly pessimistic, and tighter certificates may require additional structure (e.g. Lipschitz information) on the base model; (4) absorbing/masking kernels degenerate for suffix attacks (certified bound at most beta^d, decaying exponentially with d), so the paper adopts uniform replacement kernels instead; (5) interaction-only certification has limited coverage on broad, non-curated multimodal-safety benchmarks (only 7.5% of MM-SafetyBench qualifies); (6) the empirical adaptive attack in Section 5.3 estimates attack strength only, not certification confidence, and is not a formal soundness proof.

datasets_used: UCI ADULT census-income dataset (Becker & Kohavi, 1996; Kohavi, 1996) - tabular sanity check
Hateful Memes dataset (Kiela et al., 2020), filtered into a 400-example interaction-only subset using LLaVA-Guard and FLUX.1-Kontext text removal
MM-SafetyBench (Liu et al., 2025), 1,680 samples, external benchmark check, only 7.5% satisfy the interaction-only filter
quantitative_results: Table 1 (suffix attack, 100-example subset): Image-only RS radius=3.99, text budget=0; Text-only RS radius=0, text budget=3.26; Hybrid RS radius=3.76 (at d=1), text budget=3.07
ell_0 text attack: mean certified text budget d_txt=1.020 (text-only) vs d_hybrid=0.33 (hybrid)

Table 2 (beta sweep, sigma=1.0): beta=0.1 -> 82.35% coverage, mean d_max=2.29, mean radius=4.99; beta=0.25 -> 70.59% coverage, mean d_max=3.07, mean radius=3.21; beta=0.5 -> 58.82% coverage, mean d_max=4.00, mean radius=3.24; beta=1.0 -> 41.18% coverage, mean d_max=8.00, mean radius=4.57
Figure 4: at epsilon=0.5, d=3, certified accuracy = 38% (sigma=0.5) vs 36% (sigma=1.0); certification nearly vacuous for d>=5, and vacuous for d>3 at sigma=1.0
MM-SafetyBench subset: mean certified text budget=3.62, mean certified image radius=3.37

Table 4 (adaptive attack, 20 examples x 20 iterations, n=1000): Safe% = 74.286+-4.695 at (d*,r*); 75.321+-4.672 at (d*,1.5r*); 76.571+-4.730 at (d*,3r*); 76.393+-5.123 at (d*+2,r*); 90.214+-2.284 at (d*+4,r*) -- all far below the ~99.995% needed to violate tau=4.6e-5

Table 3 (runtime, H100, n=10,000): image-only RS ~156s; hybrid RS default ~500s; hybrid RS + batching/FlashAttention ~0.7x of default; one-shot suffix/ell_0 (d_max=8) ~44s with radius degrading 2.07->1.55 and budget 1.48->1.23; text-only RS one-shot ~44s
Tabular ADULT: certified accuracy >30% up to epsilon=1.9 at d=0; no certification beyond epsilon=0.75 at d=2
baselines_compared: Image-only randomized smoothing (Gaussian RS, Cohen et al. 2019 style)
Text-only randomized smoothing (discrete fractional-knapsack RS, following Lee et al. 2019 / Chen et al. 2025a)

MMCert-style independent per-modality subsampling baseline (Wang et al., 2024) - reported in Appendix A.10 as yielding zero certifiable examples on the interaction-only subset under independent subsampling at any keep ratio (referenced in main text but full table is in an appendix beyond the extracted 22 pages)
